title: Attention Tells When It Doesn't Know: Layerwise Entropy Profiles for Training-Free OOD Detection and Calibration in Vision Transformers
description: We propose Attention Entropy Profiling (AEP), a training-free method that extracts out-of-distribution detection scores and calibration signals from the self-attention maps of pretrained Vision Transformers. By computing a compact vector of layerwise attention entropy statistics (CLS entropy, average token entropy, attention concentration, and head agreement) and measuring its deviation from in-distribution reference statistics via Mahalanobis distance, AEP provides a zero-overhead OOD detector and input-adaptive confidence calibrator that is complementary to existing logit/feature-based approaches.
motivation: Current OOD detection methods for Vision Transformers treat the model as a black box, using only output logits or penultimate-layer features. They completely ignore the self-attention maps — the defining mechanism of transformers — which encode rich information about how the model distributes computational focus across spatial tokens at each layer. Meanwhile, post-hoc calibration methods (temperature scaling) use a global scalar and degrade under distribution shift. There is a clear gap: no method exploits the layerwise attention structure of ViTs for OOD detection or distribution-shift-aware calibration, despite attention maps being computed for free during inference.
proposed_approach: AEP extracts four attention statistics at each layer and head: (1) CLS attention entropy measuring classification-relevant focus, (2) average token entropy measuring global attention diffuseness, (3) attention concentration ratio measuring top-k token dominance, and (4) head agreement score measuring inter-head consensus. These are aggregated into a compact 5L-dimensional profile (L=number of layers). In-distribution statistics (mean and covariance) are computed from ~1000 calibration images. At test time, the Mahalanobis distance of a new input's profile from ID statistics serves as an OOD score. For calibration, this score drives an input-adaptive temperature that softens predictions for anomalous inputs while preserving sharpness for normal ones. AEP is complementary to logit-based scores and can be fused with them for improved performance.

related_work.title: A Baseline for Detecting Misclassified and Out-of-Distribution Examples in Neural Networks
related_work.relation: Foundational MSP baseline for OOD detection using softmax probabilities. AEP provides an orthogonal signal from attention structure rather than output probabilities.

related_work.title: Energy-based Out-of-distribution Detection
related_work.authors: Weitang Liu, Xiaoyun Wang, John Owens, Yixuan Li
related_work.relation: State-of-the-art logit-based OOD score. AEP captures attention-level information complementary to energy scores and can be fused with them.

related_work.title: ViM: Out-Of-Distribution with Virtual-logit Matching
related_work.authors: Haoqi Wang, Zhizhong Li, Litong Feng, Wayne Zhang
related_work.relation: Feature-based OOD detection using virtual logits. One of the strongest post-hoc methods; AEP provides complementary attention-based signal.

related_work.title: Extremely Simple Activation Shaping for Out-of-Distribution Detection
related_work.authors: Andrija Djurisic, Nebojsa Bae, Wai Sum Lie, Zongjian Liu
related_work.relation: Activation-based OOD detection. Operates on feature activations, not attention structure; complementary to AEP.

related_work.title: On Calibration of Modern Neural Networks
related_work.authors: Chuan Guo, Geoff Pleiss, Yu Sun, Kilian Q. Weinberger
related_work.relation: Foundational work on temperature scaling for calibration. Uses global temperature; AEP extends this with input-adaptive temperature driven by attention entropy.

related_work.title: Calibration Attention: Learning Reliability-Aware Representations for Vision Transformers
related_work.authors: Wenhao Liang, Wei Emma Zhang, Lin Yue, Miao Xu, Mingyu Guo, Olaf Maennel, Weitong Chen
related_work.relation: Most closely related work — learns instance-specific temperature from CLS token. Key difference: CalAttn requires supervised training; AEP is completely training-free and uses attention entropy profiles rather than learned representations.

related_work.title: Is Less More? Exploring Token Condensation as Training-free Test-time Adaptation
related_work.authors: Zixin Wang, Dong Gong, Sen Wang, Zi Huang, Yadan Luo
related_work.relation: Uses token condensation for test-time adaptation. Related in using token-level information for adaptation, but serves a different purpose (accuracy improvement vs. OOD detection/calibration).

related_work.title: Attention Head Entropy of LLMs Predicts Answer Correctness
related_work.authors: Sophie Ostmeier, Brian Axelrod, Maya Varma, Asad Aali, Yabin Zhang, Magdalini Paschali, Sanmi Koyejo, Curtis Langlotz, Akshay Chaudhari
related_work.relation: Uses attention head entropy in LLMs to predict correctness. Inspirational but for NLP only; we extend the concept to vision transformers for OOD detection and calibration with a different methodology.

related_work.title: Entropy-Lens: The Information Signature of Transformer Computations
related_work.relation: Analyzes entropy of intermediate predictions in LLMs for interpretability. Focuses on language models and interpretability; we focus on vision transformers and practical OOD/calibration applications.

related_work.title: OpenOOD v1.5: Enhanced Benchmark for Out-of-Distribution Detection
related_work.authors: Jingyang Zhang, Jingkang Yang, Pengyun Wang, et al.
related_work.relation: Standard benchmark we will use for evaluation. Provides comprehensive comparison of 40+ OOD detection methods on ImageNet-scale.

related_work.title: An Image is Worth 16x16 Words: Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale
related_work.authors: Alexey Dosovitskiy, Lucas Beyer, Alexander Kolesnikov, et al.
related_work.relation: Original Vision Transformer paper. AEP is designed to work with ViT and its variants.

related_work.title: Can You Trust Your Model's Uncertainty? Evaluating Predictive Uncertainty Under Dataset Shift
related_work.authors: Yaniv Ovadia, Emily Fertig, Jie Ren, et al.
related_work.relation: Shows calibration methods degrade under distribution shift. Directly motivates AEP's input-adaptive calibration approach.

hypothesis: The layerwise attention entropy profile of a Vision Transformer — capturing how attention entropy, concentration, and head agreement vary across layers — deviates systematically for out-of-distribution inputs compared to in-distribution inputs. This deviation, measured via Mahalanobis distance from in-distribution statistics, provides a competitive OOD detection signal and enables input-adaptive confidence calibration that is more robust to distribution shift than global temperature scaling.

confirms_hypothesis: AEP-Score achieves AUROC within 2% of the best post-hoc baseline on at least 2 of 5 OOD benchmarks
Fusing AEP with the best logit/feature-based method outperforms either alone (average AUROC improvement >= 0.5%)
AEP-based adaptive calibration reduces ECE by >= 10% relative to temperature scaling on ImageNet-C severity 5
Layerwise entropy profiles show statistically significant differences between ID and OOD (p < 0.01)
refutes_hypothesis: AEP-Score AUROC is consistently >5% below the best baseline across all OOD benchmarks
No improvement from fusion with existing methods on any benchmark
Entropy profiles do not significantly differ between ID and OOD inputs
AEP-based calibration performs no better than global temperature scaling under any shift
